mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that helps you identify vulnerabilities in your container images and running workloads. It automates the process of scanning images and provides insights into security issues, making it essential for maintaining a secure Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include the need for additional resources and potential performance impacts during scans.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Aqua Security Helm repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator from it.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Aqua Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Trivy Operator CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for the Trivy Operator to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aquasecurity/trivy-operator/main/deploy/cluster-scanner/crds.yaml

```

> This command applies the necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Trivy Operator using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator in the specified namespace, ensuring that you are using a specific version.

```bash

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --create-namespace --version 0.23.0

```

> Replace '0.23.0' with the desired version if needed.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) to initiate scanning of your workloads.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: scan.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: ClusterScan
metadata:
  name: example-clusterscan
spec:
  scan:
    targets:
      - kind: Pod
        name: example-pod
EOF

```

> This command creates a ClusterScan resource targeting a specific pod.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: scan.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: ClusterScan
metadata:
  name: example-clusterscan
spec:
  scan:
    targets:
      - kind: Pod
        name: example-pod
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the created Custom Resource to ensure it is ready and scanning.

```bash

kubectl get clusterscan example-clusterscan -o yaml

```

> This command outputs the details of the ClusterScan resource.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that any scans and results will be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> This command uninstalls the Trivy Operator.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup any important configurations. This step will upgrade the Trivy Operator to a new version.

```bash

kubectl get clusterscan --all-namespaces -o yaml > clusterscan-backup.yaml

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.1

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backup existing ClusterScan resources.
> Replace '0.23.1' with the desired version.
> Verify that the Trivy Operator pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "ClusterScan" in version "scan.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the CRDs for Trivy Operator have not been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aquasecurity/trivy-operator/main/deploy/cluster-scanner/crds.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Trivy Operator pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration or insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Scan results are not appearing.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClusterScan resource may not be targeting any workloads or may be misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Ensure the ClusterScan resource is correctly configured and targets existing workloads.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the scan is taking too long and timing out.

**Fix:**
Increase the timeout settings in the ClusterScan resource or check for performance issues in the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for additional scan targets, improved performance of scanning operations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of the ClusterScan resource with the status 'Ready' and the scan results populated.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: scan.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: ClusterScan
metadata:
  name: example-clusterscan
spec:
  scan:
    targets:
      - kind: Pod
        name: example-pod
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
